summary: postinstall script exfiltrates npm auth tokens and host metadata to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes shell commands that read `.npmrc` files at `/home/node/.npmrc`, `/setup/node/.npmrc`, `/setup/.npmrc`, and `/usr/lib/node_modules/npm/.npmrc`, and greps recursively for `_authToken=` values and other secrets across `/setup`, `/home/node`, and `/usr/lib/node_modules/npm`. It also collects `/etc/hosts`, mountinfo, `/readflag` output, and home directory listings. The captured data is base64-wrapped inside a fake YAML 'manifest' and PUT to `http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-*` over cleartext HTTP. The script gates execution to evade scanners: it bails out when CI environment variables (`GITHUB_ACTIONS`, `RUNNER_OS`, `CI`) are present, when the npm registry resolves to common Chinese mirrors (`npmmirror`, `tencent`, `cnpm`, `taobao`, etc.), or when the cwd is `/tmp`; it only proceeds when the hostname matches a 12-hex-character pattern and cwd includes `/app`. The hardcoded IP `154.57.164.64:32271` is attacker-controlled infrastructure with no relation to any legitimate package registry or CDN. Combined behavior — environment-aware dormancy, npm token harvesting, and HTTP exfiltration to a bare-IP endpoint — is an unambiguous credential-theft payload that compromises any npm publishing identity present on the install host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
